title: The Village
description: The Village — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "The Village" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: "Table of Contents"
memes.description: "Table of Contents for The Village"

memes.label: "Akim"
memes.description: "A Durnovka peasant and night watchman afflicted with night-blindness — a figure of mockingly sceptical intelligence whose rough vitality and sardonic wit represent the unvarnished rural character that Bunin depicts without sentimentality."

memes.label: "Alcohol and Violence"
memes.description: "The entwined phenomena of alcohol abuse and casual violence that pervade the world of The Village — presented by Bunin not as moral failings but as structural consequences of rural degradation."

memes.label: "Autobiographical Note"
memes.description: "Autobiographical note by Ivan Bunin"

memes.label: "Balashkin"
memes.description: "An intellectual acquaintance of Kuzma Krasoff — representing the educated class's engagement with and estrangement from the Russian peasant question."

memes.label: "Bread and Sustenance"
memes.description: "Bread as the central symbol of survival, dignity, and community in The Village — the most basic measure of human need and the recurring test of human charity."

memes.label: "Cavalry Captain Durnovo"
memes.description: "The serf-owner who hunted the Krasoffs' great-grandfather with wolfhounds — the ancestral figure of gentry violence whose act defines the moral universe of The Village."

memes.label: Children and Childhood
memes.description: The experience of childhood and the place of children in the village world of Bunin's The Village — childhood as a distinct life stage shaped by labor, family, and limited horizons.

memes.label: "Colophon"
memes.description: "Typography and production notes"

memes.label: "Commerce and Trade"
memes.description: "The world of rural commerce in The Village — fairs, peddling, shopkeeping, and the brutal economic logic that transforms the Krasoff brothers from petty hucksters' sons into participants in the remaking of rural Russia."

memes.label: Concept Index
memes.description: Complete index of all concept entries in The Village wiki, covering themes, ideas, customs, and social phenomena explored in the novel.

memes.label: "Death and Mortality"
memes.description: "The omnipresence of death in The Village — not as exceptional event but as the constant companion of rural life, shaping consciousness and measuring the value of existence."

memes.label: "Deniska"
memes.description: "Son of Syery the Grey Man and a young peasant cobbler of Durnovka — a representative of the post-emancipation generation whose vitality is channelled into cruelty, cunning, and casual amorality, embodying Bunin's bleakest vision of the Russian peasantry's future."

memes.label: Donka the Goat
memes.description: A village girl in Durnovka, nicknamed "the Goat" — pretty, stupid, and energetic, she is the Bride's only friend and the sole witness to her brutal humiliation.

memes.label: "Durnovka"
memes.description: "The fictional village at the center of The Village — named for the Russian durnoy ('bad, foul'), Durnovka is both a specific estate owned by Tikhon Krasoff and a synecdoche for the degradation, resilience, and moral complexity of rural Russia after emancipation."

memes.label: "Education and Literacy"
memes.description: "The role of education and literacy in The Village — Kuzma's self-taught intellectualism as both escape from and confrontation with the brutal illiteracy of rural Russia."

memes.label: Eletz
memes.description: A real trading town in the Oryol region where Tikhon Krasoff worked as a drover's assistant and clerk, and near which several notable incidents in the novel occur, including the Bykoff brothers' farm tragedy and a distillery accident.

memes.label: "Endnotes"
memes.description: "Endnotes for the novel"

memes.label: Event Index
memes.description: Complete index of all event entries in The Village wiki, covering historical and in-novel events that shape the characters' world.

memes.label: "Faith and Religion"
memes.description: "The role of Orthodox Christianity, popular piety, and religious crisis in The Village — a world where faith coexists with brutality, and religious longing persists amid spiritual decay."

memes.label: "Family and Kinship"
memes.description: "The family as both refuge and prison in The Village — the bonds of blood that sustain the Krasoffs and the violence that those same bonds generate."

memes.label: "Famine and Hunger"
memes.description: "The recurring catastrophe of famine in rural Russia — a defining feature of peasant life that Bunin presents not as exceptional disaster but as the normal condition of existence in The Village."

memes.label: "Fate and Fortune"
memes.description: "The role of fatalism, fortune-telling, and belief in destiny in The Village; a worldview in which human agency is circumscribed by forces beyond control."

memes.label: "Fear and Violence"
memes.description: "The role of fear as a mechanism of social control and the pervasive everyday violence in The Village; from landlord's whip to husband's fist."

memes.label: Folk Song and Music
memes.description: The role of peasant songs, singing, and vernacular musical culture as expressions of village emotion and social life in The Village.

memes.label: Horses and Livestock
memes.description: The central role of horses and livestock in peasant economy and daily life in Bunin's The Village — as trade goods, transportation, status markers, and companions in labor.

memes.label: "Imprint"
memes.description: "Publishing imprint and copyright information"

memes.label: "Ivanushka"
memes.description: "A figure from the Krasoffs' family past — the great-grandfather's generation whose story of serfdom and survival haunts the novel's present."

memes.label: "Justice and Authority"
memes.description: "The structures of authority and the absence of justice in The Village — where law exists to extract taxes and enforce hierarchy, not to protect the weak or punish the powerful."

memes.label: Kazakovo
memes.description: A village and gentry estate located twelve versts from Durnovka, site of the manor-park and orchard that Kuzma visits in Part II, and where he confronts the futility of his dreams of rural life.

memes.label: Komar
memes.description: One of the chief peasant rioters of Durnovka, renowned for his strength, cleverness, and daring in confronting the gentry, whose death in a distillery accident is treated by the narrator as fate.

memes.label: Koshel
memes.description: A farmhand on Tikhon Ilitch's estate in The Village — a practical, rough-spoken peasant who embodies the self-interest and survival instinct of the rural laboring class.

memes.label: "Kuzma Krasoff"
memes.description: "The younger Krasoff brother — a self-educated peasant intellectual, failed writer, and reluctant estate manager whose sensitivity and moral seriousness isolate him from his class, making him the novel's tormented conscience and Bunin's vehicle for the deepest questions about the Russian condition."

memes.label: "Labor and Toil"
memes.description: "The physical labor that defines peasant existence in The Village — its rhythms, its dignity, and its brutality — as both the material foundation of rural life and a marker of social degradation."

memes.label: "Land Ownership"
memes.description: "The struggle over land in Bunin's The Village — the economic foundation of rural Russian society, where sovereignty over the soil determines status, identity, and the boundary between survival and destitution."

memes.label: Lipetzk
memes.description: A trading village in the Oryol region where Tikhon Krasoff worked in a candle shop and where the gardener Pankoff resided; also the place of origin of Akim's family.

memes.label: Makar the Pilgrim
memes.description: A wandering pilgrim and holy fool who appears in Durnovka, known for his prophetic pronouncements and the unsettling effect he has on the villagers.

memes.label: "Makarka"
memes.description: "A wandering pilgrim and holy fool figure in The Village — representing the religious underside of rural life, where piety and madness blur."

memes.label: "Matorin"
memes.description: "The shopkeeper under whom the young Krasoff brothers worked — a figure of the established merchant class against which Tikhon's later success can be measured."

memes.label: "Matorin's Shop"
memes.description: "The bazaar shop where the young Tikhon and Kuzma worked as assistants — a training ground in the commercial arts that would shape their divergent destinies."

memes.label: "Memory and the Past"
memes.description: "Memory as a narrative and psychological force in The Village — the mechanism through which characters measure their present degradation against a lost world of childhood, youth, and imagined better times."

memes.label: "Menshoff"
memes.description: "A peasant from Durnovka who drives Kuzma Krasoff around the village and provides local commentary in Part II of Bunin's The Village."

memes.label: "Mitrofan"
memes.description: "A stern, opinionated peasant watchman who appears in the night scene at the bathhouse in Part II of Bunin's The Village, debating poverty with Akim and the baker."

memes.label: Modernity and Change
memes.description: The encroachment of modern political, economic, and technological forces on traditional village life in The Village.

memes.label: "Moscow"
memes.description: "The historic capital of Russia, referenced in the novel as a distant, unreachable symbol of the wider world beyond provincial life."

memes.label: Mount Athos
memes.description: The holy mountain and monastic republic in Greece, from which Tikhon Krasoff receives a begging letter — a touchstone for his complicated, transactional relationship with Orthodox faith.

memes.label: "Nastasya Petrovna"
memes.description: "The sister-in-law of the Krasoff brothers — a vodka-pouring hostess whose body, defined by incessant pregnancies ending in dead girl-babies, embodies the brutal intersection of gender, fertility, and rural degradation."

memes.label: "Nature and Landscape"
memes.description: "The Russian natural world as depicted in The Village — not merely a backdrop but an active force that shapes character, mood, and meaning in Bunin's rural universe."

memes.label: "Night and Darkness"
memes.description: "The structural role of night and darkness in The Village — as a space of danger, intimacy, and revelation that contrasts with the daylight world of labor and social hierarchy."

memes.label: Organization Index
memes.description: Complete index of all organization entries in The Village wiki, covering institutions, government bodies, businesses, and religious organizations referenced in the novel.

memes.label: Person Index
memes.description: Complete index of all person entries in The Village wiki, sorted alphabetically by name.

memes.label: Place Index
memes.description: Complete index of all place entries in The Village wiki, covering real and fictional locations from the novel.

memes.label: Roads and Travel
memes.description: The road as a physical and symbolic space in Bunin's The Village — connecting Durnovka to the outside world, a site of danger and commerce, and a metaphor for the peasant's condition.

memes.label: "Rodka"
memes.description: "A tall, thin, sullen young peasant from Ulianovka who marries the Bride (Molodaya) and becomes a symbol of brutal peasant domestic life in Bunin's The Village."

memes.label: "Rural Poverty"
memes.description: "The pervasive material deprivation and economic stagnation of rural Russia, depicted by Bunin as the defining condition of peasant life in The Village and the source of the Russian peasant's moral and spiritual degradation."

memes.label: "Russian Soul"
memes.description: "The 'Russian soul' as explored by Bunin in The Village — the peculiar complexity, contradictory depths, and tragic nature of the Russian national character, depicted without idealization or political agenda."

memes.label: "The Russo-Turkish War (1877–78)"
memes.description: "The tenth Russo-Turkish War — a conflict fought between the Russian Empire and the Ottoman Empire primarily over the Balkans, referenced in The Village through character memory, popular print culture, and the historical consciousness of a generation for whom this war defined Russian military glory."

memes.label: Sakharoff
memes.description: The chief of the post-office at Ulianovka — a savage-tempered official who terrorizes peasants and embodies the petty tyranny of rural bureaucracy.

memes.label: "Serfdom"
memes.description: "The institution of serfdom and its enduring legacy in rural Russia — abolished only forty-five years before the events of The Village, yet its social and psychological effects pervade every aspect of village life."

memes.label: "Sery (Gray)"
memes.description: "A Durnovka peasant nicknamed 'Gray' — his very name suggesting the colorless, featureless quality of rural poverty that erases individuality."

memes.label: "Social Class"
memes.description: "The rigid yet shifting social hierarchies of rural Russia depicted in The Village — the decay of the landed gentry, the rise of the merchant class, and the immobility of the peasantry."

memes.label: "Soldiers and Conscription"
memes.description: "The institution of military service in The Village; a pathway out of rural life, a source of violence and experience, and a lens through which Bunin examines Russian society."

memes.label: "Standard Ebooks"
memes.description: "The volunteer-driven digital publisher that produced the modern ebook edition of The Village — representing the contemporary institutional framework for preserving and distributing public domain literature."

memes.label: Superstition and Folk Belief
memes.description: The persistence of pre-Christian folk beliefs, omens, and supernatural practices among the peasantry in The Village.

memes.label: Syery
memes.description: A poor peasant of Durnovka, nicknamed "the Grey Man," whose aimless life, grievance-collecting, and parasitic social habits exemplify the spiritual decay Bunin diagnoses in rural Russia.

memes.label: "The Imperial Russian Army"
memes.description: "The military institution that conscripted peasants and served as an instrument of state power — a shadow presence in the novel."

memes.label: The Bathhouse
memes.description: An old bathhouse in the garden of the Durnovka estate where Kuzma Krasoff spends a night during his visit to the manor, situated among the apple trees at the far end of the orchard.

memes.label: "The Black Suburb"
memes.description: "The poorest district of the Old Town — named for the soot and filth that cover it, a geographical marker of the social stratification that Bunin maps onto urban space."

memes.label: The Bride
memes.description: The unnamed rural woman — known only as "The Bride" — whom Tikhon Ilitch forces into marriage with Deniska in the novel's climactic sequence, representing the powerlessness of peasant women.

memes.label: "The Cathedral of St. Sophia"
memes.description: "The great cathedral in the Old Town where Kuzma stands during the Liturgy — a monument of institutional Orthodoxy observed by a peasant who cannot find answers within its walls."

memes.label: The Caucasus
memes.description: A mountainous region in southern Russia that represents an unrealized dream of escape for Kuzma Krasoff, who once planned to emigrate there with the Dukhobortzy, and where Akim served as a soldier but could recall nothing of the place beyond the mountains.

memes.label: "The Cemetery"
memes.description: "The burial ground where the Krasoff family dead rest — a place of memory and peace that offers the dignity that life in Durnovka denies."

memes.label: "The Cemetery Visit"
memes.description: "Tikhon Ilitch's visit to the family graves during his illness — a moment when commerce yields to memory and the hard merchant is moved by the inscriptions of the dead."

memes.label: "The Church of Durnovka"
memes.description: "The Orthodox church that anchors the spiritual and temporal rhythm of village life — a constant but insufficient presence against rural degradation."

memes.label: "The Court System"
memes.description: "The judicial institutions of rural Russia — where justice was administered by gentry magistrates and peasant elders under a legal code that favored the propertied."

memes.label: "The Death of the Little Sister"
memes.description: "The childhood death of the Krasoff sister — a formative loss that haunts both brothers and draws Tikhon back to the family graves."

memes.label: "The State Duma"
memes.description: "The elected legislative assembly of the Russian Empire (1906–1917) — a recurring topic of conversation, hope, and disillusionment among the peasants and merchants of The Village, representing both the promise and the failure of constitutional reform in late imperial Russia."

memes.label: "The Dumb Cook"
memes.description: "Tikhon Ilitch's mute servant and mistress — a figure of absolute voicelessness whose body and child embody the intersection of class exploitation and gender subordination."

memes.label: "The Emancipation of the Serfs (1861)"
memes.description: "The abolition of serfdom by Tsar Alexander II — the historical event whose incomplete legacy shapes every aspect of the world depicted in The Village."

memes.label: "The Fair and Bazaar"
memes.description: "The annual fair held on the town pasture — the novel's great set piece of commercial and social life, where every class and type of rural Russia converges in a 'dull, discordant roar.'"

memes.label: "The Family Reconciliation"
memes.description: "The uneasy reunion of Tikhon and Kuzma after years of estrangement — the novel's closest approach to a redemptive arc."

memes.label: "The Famine"
memes.description: "The recurring catastrophe of crop failure and mass hunger in rural Russia — in The Village, famine is not an exceptional disaster but the endemic condition of peasant life, a seasonal visitor whose return is as certain as winter and as devastating as war."

memes.label: "The Fire"
memes.description: "A destructive village fire — one of the recurring disasters that punctuate rural life, alongside famine, epidemic, and tax enforcement."

memes.label: "The Fratricidal Dispute"
memes.description: "The near-fatal knife fight between Tikhon and Kuzma over peddling profits — the founding trauma of the Krasoff family narrative."

memes.label: The Garden
memes.description: The garden at the Durnovka manor — a site of labor, illicit romance, and the Bride's suicide, framed by the tension between its pastoral beauty and the harshness of peasant life.

memes.label: "The Gentry"
memes.description: "The declining landowning class of rural Russia in The Village — a once-dominant estate reduced to poverty and irrelevance, whose decay is both economic fact and moral symbol."

memes.label: "The Gentry Assembly"
memes.description: "The corporate body of the landed nobility — a once-dominant institution in terminal decline, losing land and influence to the merchant class."

memes.label: "The Great Fair"
memes.description: "The annual commercial gathering on the town pasture — a temporary city where rural Russia displays its full spectrum of commerce, class, and chaos."

memes.label: The Harness-Maker
memes.description: A "vicious emaciated fellow" from Durnovka, one of the chief organizers of the peasant attack on Tikhon Krasoff's manor, whose brazen return to Tikhon's shop afterward illustrates the brazen impunity of the rebels.

memes.label: "The Inn"
memes.description: "A corner lodging-house in the provincial town where Kuzma Krasoff spends a long, desolate summer, emblematic of his poverty and isolation."

memes.label: "The Journey to Kiev"
memes.description: "Kuzma's pilgrimage-like journey to Kiev — a movement through Russian space that is also a movement through Russian history and identity."

memes.label: "The Krasoff Estate"
memes.description: "The manor and lands acquired by Tikhon Ilitch — the physical embodiment of his ascent from peddler to landowner, and the ancestral violence that stains his property."

memes.label: "The Krasoff Family Enterprise"
memes.description: "The peddling and trading operation run by the Krasoff brothers — an informal economic unit that is simultaneously a family business and a site of fraternal violence."

memes.label: "The Marriage of Tikhon Ilitch"
memes.description: "The marriage that Tikhon contemplates and arranges — an economic transaction as much as a personal union, reflecting the commodification of all relationships in the rural economy."

memes.label: "The Merchant Class"
memes.description: "The rising commercial class represented by Tikhon Krasoff — buyers, sellers, and speculators remaking the rural economy through acquisition."

memes.label: "The Old Town"
memes.description: "The unnamed provincial town where the annual fair is held — a hub of commerce, vice, and social mixing that reveals the full spectrum of rural Russian life."

memes.label: The Orchard
memes.description: The cherry orchard at Durnovka and Kazakovo — a site of peasant destruction during the uprising, Kuzma's shattered pastoral dreams, and the Bride's tragic encounter, framed by the cycle of seasons from blossoming to barren winter.

memes.label: "The Orthodox Church"
memes.description: "The Russian Orthodox Church as an institution in The Village — provider of ritual framework, social hierarchy, and spiritual consolation in a world where faith coexists uneasily with superstition, violence, and the materialism of the rising merchant class."

memes.label: "The Peasantry"
memes.description: "The Russian peasantry as the central subject of Bunin's The Village — a raw, unsparing portrait of rural life, poverty, resilience, and moral complexity in the decades following the Emancipation of 1861."

memes.label: "The Pilgrim"
memes.description: "A wandering religious pilgrim who appears in the novel — part of the tradition of Russian spiritual wandering that offers an alternative to both institutional religion and rural degradation."

memes.label: "The Pilgrim Community"
memes.description: "The informal network of religious wanderers (stranniki) who moved through rural Russia — a decentralized spiritual institution operating outside the Orthodox hierarchy."

memes.label: "The Pilgrimage"
memes.description: "The wandering religious journey undertaken by pilgrims like Makarka — a form of spiritual mobility available to those who renounce fixed habitation."

memes.label: The Pond
memes.description: The pond at Durnovka — a clayey water source for cattle, a site of village drama when the ice breaks, and a recurring image of the squalor and endurance of peasant life through the long winter.

memes.label: The Post Office
memes.description: The post office at Ulianovka run by the fearsome Sakharoff — a bureaucratic outpost in the wilds that serves as Durnovka's tenuous connection to the outside world through newspapers and letters.

memes.label: "The Pothouse"
memes.description: "The drinking establishment the Krasoff father ran in Durnovka — a site where alcohol lubricates the dissolution of family and community."

memes.label: "The Railway Station"
memes.description: "The railway station near Vorgol and Durnovka, a crossroads of peasant life, commerce, and transit where key encounters between characters take place."

memes.label: "The River"
memes.description: "The shallow river dividing the Old Town from the Black Suburb — a geographical and social threshold marked by the stench of the tanyards."

memes.label: "The Road"
memes.description: "The roads connecting Durnovka to the Old Town and the wider world — arteries of commerce and migration that promise escape but deliver only more of the same."

memes.label: "The Rural Police"
memes.description: "The uriadniki who enforce tax collection and maintain order in the district — the visible arm of state authority in rural Russia."

memes.label: "The Russian Academy of Sciences"
memes.description: "The Imperial Academy that elected Bunin an Honorary Academician in 1909 — representing the institutional recognition of literature in late imperial Russia."

memes.label: "The Russian Revolution of 1905"
memes.description: "The failed revolution whose aftermath of reaction and stagnation forms the political atmosphere in which Bunin wrote The Village."

memes.label: The Schoolhouse
memes.description: A whitewashed school building gleaming on the pasture beyond Durnovka village, visible from the manor-house porch alongside the windmill, visited by Kuzma Krasoff on a fresh sunny morning.

memes.label: "The State Vodka Monopoly"
memes.description: "The government monopoly on vodka production and sale (1894–1902) — making the state the largest purveyor of spirits in the empire and the indirect cause of rural alcoholism."

memes.label: The Steppe
memes.description: The vast, open plain surrounding Durnovka and dominating the landscape of The Village, functioning as both a physical setting and a central symbol of the novel's thematic concerns.

memes.label: "The Tanyards"
memes.description: "The leather-tanning district along the river in the Old Town — a zone of industrial stench that marks the boundary between town and suburb, commerce and poverty."

memes.label: "The Tax Authority"
memes.description: "The state fiscal apparatus that extracted revenue from the peasantry — the institutional engine of rural immiseration."

memes.label: "The Tax Collection"
memes.description: "The recurring autumnal event when rural police extract taxes from the peasantry — the engine of rural dispossession and the rhythm of Tikhon Krasoff's commercial calendar."

memes.label: "The Threshing Floor"
memes.description: "The open space where grain is separated from chaff — a site of agricultural labor where Kuzma sits and contemplates the Russian people."

memes.label: "The Two Russias: Tikhon and Kuzma Krasoff"
memes.description: "An analysis of the Krasoff brothers as Bunin's dialectical portrait of the Russian character — Tikhon the doer and Kuzma the thinker, the merchant and the poet, the man of land and the man of books, whose contrasting fates reveal the irreconcilable tensions at the heart of post-emancipation rural Russia."

memes.label: "The Peasant Uprising"
memes.description: "The coordinated peasant uprising that swept through the county one summer — at Durnovka, the peasants attacked Tikhon Ilitch's manor, shouting demands and setting fire to the watchman's hut, an event that terrified the landowner class but proved more noise than substance."

memes.label: "The Village Commune (Mir)"
memes.description: "The traditional institution of collective land tenure and local self-governance in rural Russia — the mir (мир) that distributed land, apportioned taxes, and exercised social control over peasant life, whose values of communal obligation and collective responsibility persist as an invisible architecture long after its formal powers had eroded."

memes.label: "The Village Fair"
memes.description: "The seasonal fair that punctuates rural time — a moment of economic and social intensity that reveals the structures of village life more clearly than any ordinary day."

memes.label: "The Violence Cycle: Alcohol, Fear, and Rebellion"
memes.description: "An analysis of the causal chain connecting alcohol, fear, and violence in The Village — a self-reinforcing cycle that Bunin presents as the engine of rural social life, whereby drinking fuels aggression, aggression breeds fear, and fear drives further drinking, punctuated by periodic eruptions of collective violence."

memes.label: "The Wolf Encounter"
memes.description: "The hand-to-hand fight between Little Russian peasants and a mad wolf — a moment of primal violence that connects the present to ancient peasant life."

memes.label: "The Zemstvo"
memes.description: "The institution of local self-government created by the 1864 reforms — one of the few channels for peasant participation in public life."

memes.label: "Tikhon Ilitch Krasoff"
memes.description: "The elder Krasoff brother — a peasant-turned-merchant whose ruthless commercial ascent from peddler to landowner embodies the post-emancipation transformation of rural Russia, and whose spiritual desolation reveals its human cost."

memes.label: "Titlepage"
memes.description: "Title page of the book"

memes.label: Town and City
memes.description: The representation of the provincial town and the city as sites of commerce, culture, and alienation from rural life in The Village.

memes.label: Trading Street
memes.description: A paved commercial street in an unnamed provincial town (likely Voronezh), evoked by Kuzma as a sensory memory of urban pleasure — bakeshops, fresh rolls, and the mechanical organ of the Kars eating-house.

memes.label: "Truth and Falsehood"
memes.description: "The pervasive tension between truth and deception in The Village; a world where pious words mask cruel realities and honest feeling struggles against habitual dishonesty."

memes.label: "Tula"
memes.description: "An industrial city south of Moscow, referenced in the novel as Deniska's ill-fated destination and as the site of Rusanoff's estate where Syery briefly works."

memes.label: "Ulianovka"
memes.description: "A village near Durnovka, home to the deacon Govoroff and postmaster Sakharoff, serving as a local hub for travellers and postal services."

memes.label: "Uncopyright"
memes.description: "Public domain dedication"

memes.label: "Vanka"
memes.description: "A young Durnovka peasant whose restless energy and rootlessness embody the post-emancipation generation's disconnection from both land and tradition."

memes.label: Vanka Krasny
memes.description: A Durnovka peasant and rebel, twice imprisoned, whose loud-voiced wife and flight to the Donetz mines bracket his brief and violent presence in the novel.

memes.label: Vorgol
memes.description: Posting-station village on the metalled highway, five versts from Durnovka, where Tikhon Krasoff operated a dram-shop and shop — his commercial base after parting from his brother Kuzma.

memes.label: "Voronezh"
memes.description: "The provincial capital of Voronezh Governorate in central Russia — Ivan Bunin's birthplace in 1870 and a recurring geographical reference point in The Village, where the city functions as a distant horizon of culture, commerce, and escape from rural immobility."

memes.label: Wedding and Ritual
memes.description: The role of the traditional peasant wedding as a social, religious, and economic ritual in the village world of The Village.

memes.label: Winter and Seasons
memes.description: The seasonal cycle as a structuring force in peasant life in Bunin's The Village — the harshness of winter, the rhythm of agricultural seasons, and the way seasons shape character and community.

memes.label: "Women in The Village"
memes.description: "A cross-character analysis of female experience in The Village — the Bride, Nastasya Petrovna, the Dumb Cook, and other women whose lives of silent suffering, economic dependence, and reproductive tragedy offer the novel's most damning evidence against Bunin's rural world."

memes.label: "Yakoff"
memes.description: "A peasant of Durnovka whose stubborn passivity and fatalistic endurance represent one pole of the Russian peasant character — the capacity for survival without hope, action without purpose."

memes.label: Zhikhareff
memes.description: The son of a wealthy landowner, now a thoroughgoing drunkard — a figure of fallen gentility who haunts the highways and railway stations near Durnovka.
